name: The Freedom Bank Of Virginia
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Northern Virginia and DC metro area with business, personal, and mortgage banking products backed by local decision-making.
description_long: Freedom Bank of Virginia is a community-focused financial institution headquartered in Fairfax, Virginia, serving the Northern Virginia and DC metropolitan region. The bank is FDIC-insured and operates under the parent company Freedom Financial Holdings, Inc. (traded on OTCQX under ticker FDVA). The bank has demonstrated strong growth, with leadership announcing a 75% earnings surge and 73.4% net income increase year-over-year in early 2025, reflecting its expansion trajectory as a next-generation community bank. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products across three primary segments: Business Banking (including business loans and commercial lending through experienced Relationship Managers), Personal Banking (deposit and loan products for individual financial objectives), and Mortgage Banking (home purchase, refinance, and mortgage solutions). Current deposit products include Money Market Accounts at 3.25% APY and Certificates of Deposit (9-Month Flex) at 3.35% APY. The bank emphasizes easy-to-use technology, including mobile banking with free credit score monitoring and online account access. A new Tysons branch is under development to expand their Northern Virginia presence. Freedom Bank distinguishes itself through its "Entrepreneurial DNA" positioning, emphasizing exceptional service and local decision-making rather than corporate standardization. The bank actively participates in community initiatives, including Federal Home Loan Bank of Atlanta's Affordable Housing Program to expand homeownership access. Leadership changes in 2025 (new CFO Scott Clark, SVP of Sales & Marketing Joe Daniel, and board appointment of Pierre A. Abushacra) signal active management and strategic direction. The bank demonstrated responsive community support during the October 2025 government shutdown, offering fee waivers and payment extensions to affected clients. As a regional community bank, Freedom Bank's primary strength lies in personalized service and local lending expertise rather than the product breadth or technology sophistication of larger national banks. The published APY rates on deposit products are competitive but not exceptional compared to some online banks. The bank's geographic focus on Northern Virginia and DC metro limits accessibility for customers outside this region. Being a smaller regional institution means fewer branch locations and limited service options compared to national competitors, though this aligns with their community-bank positioning.
services: Mobile Banking with Free Credit Score Monitoring
Affordable Housing Program participation through FHLBank Atlanta
Emergency Support Services (fee waivers, payment extensions during shutdowns)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Strong recent financial performance with 75% earnings surge and 73.4% YoY net income growth
Local decision-making and personalized service through Relationship Managers
Free mobile banking with credit score monitoring and online account access
Active community involvement including FHLBank Atlanta Affordable Housing Program participation
Competitive deposit rates (3.25% Money Market APY, 3.35% 9-Month CD APY)
Responsive customer support (demonstrated fee waivers and payment extensions during government shutdown)
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Northern Virginia and DC metro area only
Fewer physical branch locations compared to national banks (new Tysons branch still coming soon)
Published deposit rates, while competitive, not among the highest-yield online banking alternatives
Regional bank status means less extensive product variety and service options than large national institutions
Limited information on website regarding business loan terms, rates, or specific mortgage product details
best_for: Small to mid-sized businesses in Northern Virginia and DC metro seeking relationship-based commercial lending and personalized advisory services
Northern Virginia homebuyers and refinancing clients prioritizing local mortgage expertise and community involvement
Individuals in the DC metro region seeking FDIC-insured savings accounts with personalized banking relationships
Federal employees and government contractors seeking responsive, community-oriented banking during economic disruptions
diagnosis: Freedom Bank of Virginia is best for small-to-mid-sized businesses and individuals in the Northern Virginia and DC metro area who prioritize personalized, relationship-based banking with local decision-making over the breadth of services offered by national banks. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only effectively serves Northern Virginia and DC metro customers, making it inaccessible for those outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
